questions.explanation: A consignment note is a document used in road and rail transport, listing the goods handed to the carrier for delivery and serving as a contract of carriage.

questions.question: The excess (deductible) in an insurance policy is

options.A: The total premium paid over the life of the policy
options.B: The first portion of any claim that the insured must pay themselves
options.C: An extra premium for high-risk activities
options.D: The additional commission earned by the insurance agent

questions.explanation: The excess (deductible) is the amount the insured agrees to pay towards any claim before the insurer pays. It reduces premiums and discourages small claims.

questions.question: Electronic funds transfer (EFT) refers to

options.A: The transfer of physical cash between bank branches
options.B: The movement of money from one bank account to another through electronic systems
options.C: The printing of bank statements via electronic printers
options.D: The electronic scanning of cheques for verification

questions.explanation: EFT is the electronic movement of money between bank accounts — including internet banking, ATM withdrawals, POS payments, and wire transfers.

questions.question: Issued share capital refers to

options.A: The maximum share capital allowed by the memorandum
options.B: The portion of authorised share capital that has actually been issued and sold to shareholders
options.C: Shares that have been paid for in full by shareholders
options.D: Shares reserved for future issue to employees

questions.explanation: Issued share capital is the portion of authorised share capital that has been issued (sold) to shareholders. It may be less than or equal to the authorised capital.

questions.question: A factor (mercantile agent) in trade is a middleman who

options.A: Buys goods in bulk and stores them in a warehouse
options.B: Sells goods on behalf of the principal and takes custody of the goods
options.C: Only arranges deals without handling the goods
options.D: Transports goods from the manufacturer to the retailer

questions.explanation: A factor (mercantile agent) takes physical possession of goods and sells them on behalf of the principal. Unlike a broker, the factor holds and handles the goods.

questions.question: Marine insurance covers

options.A: Only ships and vessels on the sea
options.B: Goods transported by sea, the ship, and liabilities arising from sea voyages
options.C: Goods transported by any mode of transport
options.D: Only the cargo, not the ship itself

questions.explanation: Marine insurance covers losses related to sea voyages including the hull (ship), cargo (goods in transit), and freight (carrier's revenue), as well as liabilities.

questions.question: A pro-rata (quantity) discount is given when

options.A: A buyer purchases goods above a certain minimum quantity
options.B: Payment is made before the due date
options.C: Goods purchased are proportionally below the full order quantity
options.D: A customer returns defective goods to the seller

questions.explanation: A quantity discount is given to buyers who purchase goods in larger quantities, incentivising bulk buying.

questions.question: Value Added Tax (VAT) in Nigeria is currently levied at
questions.explanation: Nigeria's VAT rate was increased from 5% to 7.5% in February 2020 by the Finance Act 2019. VAT is charged on the supply of most goods and services.

questions.question: Specialisation in production leads to

options.A: Increased boredom and absenteeism only
options.B: Increased productivity, efficiency, and development of expertise
options.C: Reduced need for machinery and technology
options.D: Greater flexibility in production

questions.explanation: Specialisation allows workers and businesses to focus on what they do best, leading to increased efficiency, skill development, time savings, and economies of scale.

questions.question: A parastatal organisation in Nigeria is

options.A: A privately owned company operating under government regulation
options.B: A government-owned corporation established by statute to perform specific commercial or public service functions
options.C: An international organisation operating in Nigeria
options.D: A cooperative society sponsored by the government

questions.explanation: A parastatal (statutory corporation) is a government-owned enterprise established by an Act of Parliament to carry out specific public or commercial functions.

questions.question: A shipping or freight agent is employed to

options.A: Manufacture goods for export on behalf of the exporter
options.B: Arrange shipment of goods and handle all shipping documentation on behalf of the exporter
options.C: Set customs duties on imported goods
options.D: Provide insurance for goods during transit

questions.explanation: A freight (shipping) agent (freight forwarder) arranges transportation, prepares shipping documents, books cargo space, and handles customs clearance on behalf of exporters or importers.

questions.question: A market overt is

options.A: An online marketplace open to all buyers and sellers
options.B: An open, publicly recognised market where goods can be bought and sold with legal protections for good-faith buyers
options.C: A black market where goods are sold illegally
options.D: A wholesale market restricted to registered traders

questions.explanation: A market overt is an open and public market. Under common law, a buyer who purchases goods in good faith in a market overt acquires good title even if the goods were stolen.

questions.question: The advertising copy refers to

options.A: A duplicate of the advertisement sent to the advertiser
options.B: The text or written content of an advertisement
options.C: The cost of reproducing an advertisement
options.D: The audience reached by an advertisement

questions.explanation: Advertising copy is the text or written content of an advertisement, including headlines, slogans, body text, and calls to action.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an advantage of international trade?

options.A: Access to goods not available domestically
options.B: Earning of foreign exchange
options.C: Dumping of substandard goods
options.D: Transfer of technology and skills

questions.explanation: Dumping — selling goods below cost in foreign markets — is a disadvantage and an unfair trade practice. The others listed are genuine advantages of international trade.

questions.question: A deed of partnership is important because it

options.A: Registers the partnership as a limited liability company
options.B: Sets out the rights, duties, and profit-sharing ratios of the partners
options.C: Allows partners to sell shares to the public
options.D: Provides government approval for the partnership

questions.explanation: A deed of partnership outlines capital contributions, profit/loss sharing ratios, roles, decision-making processes, and dissolution procedures.

questions.question: A trader marks up his goods by 40% on cost. If the cost price is ₦5,000, what is the selling price?
The SWIFT network in banking is used for

options.A: Printing bank notes on behalf of central banks
options.B: Sending and receiving secure financial messages between banks internationally
options.C: Detecting and preventing bank fraud
options.D: Calculating interest rates for commercial banks

questions.explanation: SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication) is a global network used by banks to send and receive payment instructions securely and reliably.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of a wholesaler?

options.A: Providing storage facilities
options.B: Giving credit to retailers
options.C: Selling directly to the final consumer
options.D: Breaking bulk for retailers

questions.explanation: Wholesalers sell to retailers, not to final consumers. Selling directly to the final consumer is the function of a retailer.

questions.question: A trade bloc is best described as

options.A: A physical barrier erected by a country to stop imports
options.B: A group of countries that have reduced or eliminated trade barriers among themselves
options.C: An agreement between two companies to stop competing
options.D: A government policy that bans foreign companies from investing

questions.explanation: A trade bloc is a group of countries that have agreed to reduce or remove trade barriers among themselves, e.g., ECOWAS, the European Union (EU), AfCFTA.

questions.question: A fire insurance policy covers

options.A: Only fires caused by electrical faults
options.B: Financial losses arising from damage to property caused by fire
options.C: Loss of life caused by fire accidents only
options.D: Only goods in transit that are destroyed by fire

questions.explanation: Fire insurance covers financial losses resulting from damage to property (buildings, stock, machinery) caused by fire and related perils such as explosion or lightning.

questions.question: A spot market is one in which

options.A: Goods are sold only at specific physical locations
options.B: Goods and payment are exchanged immediately at the current market price
options.C: Goods are delivered but payment is made later
options.D: Prices are set by government decree

questions.explanation: In a spot market, transactions are settled 'on the spot' — goods and payment are exchanged immediately at the current (spot) price.

questions.question: A tender is a document submitted by a supplier to

options.A: Request goods from a wholesaler
options.B: Formally offer to supply goods or services at a stated price in response to an invitation
options.C: Confirm delivery of goods to a buyer
options.D: Notify a buyer of a price change

questions.explanation: A tender is a formal offer to supply goods or services at a specified price, submitted in response to an invitation to tender (ITT). The buyer evaluates tenders and selects the best offer.

questions.question: A company is said to have separate legal personality, meaning

options.A: It must have a different name from its owners
options.B: It can sue and be sued in its own name, own property, and enter contracts independently of its owners
options.C: Its owners can never be held responsible for company debts
options.D: It must have a physical office separate from the owners' homes

questions.explanation: Separate legal personality (established in Salomon v Salomon & Co. Ltd, 1897) means the company is a distinct legal entity from its shareholders — it can own property, sue, be sued, and enter contracts in its own name.

questions.question: A housing cooperative is formed to

options.A: Invest in the stock market on behalf of members
options.B: Enable members to pool resources to build or acquire affordable housing
options.C: Provide loans to members at commercial interest rates
options.D: Produce and market agricultural goods

questions.explanation: A housing cooperative enables members to pool resources collectively to build, acquire, or manage affordable housing, reducing individual costs.

questions.question: 'Net 30' on an invoice means

options.A: A 30% discount is available for cash payment
options.B: The full invoice amount is due within 30 days of the invoice date
options.C: Goods will be delivered within 30 days
options.D: Payment can be made in 30 equal instalments

questions.explanation: 'Net 30' is a credit term meaning the buyer must pay the full invoice amount within 30 days of the invoice date, with no discount for early payment.

questions.question: Economies of scale refer to

options.A: Cost savings achieved by reducing the quality of products
options.B: Reductions in the average cost of production as output increases
options.C: Profits made from selling goods in international markets
options.D: Benefits gained from reducing the size of a business

questions.explanation: Economies of scale are cost advantages that arise when increasing the scale of production leads to lower average costs — through bulk buying, specialisation, and more efficient use of machinery.

questions.question: The lender of last resort function of a central bank means

options.A: The central bank is the last bank to give loans to the government
options.B: The central bank provides emergency funds to commercial banks facing liquidity crises
options.C: Commercial banks lend to borrowers who have been rejected by all other banks
options.D: The central bank lends only to the poorest members of society

questions.explanation: As lender of last resort, the central bank provides emergency liquidity (loans) to commercial banks that face temporary shortfalls of funds, preventing banking system collapse.

questions.question: A shopkeeper buys an article for ₦2,500 and wants to make a profit of 20% on the selling price. What should the selling price be?
questions.explanation: If profit = 20% of SP, then cost = 80% of SP. So SP = ₦2,500 ÷ 0.80 = ₦3,125.

questions.question: The principal aim of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) is to

options.A: Fund large infrastructure projects in developing countries
options.B: Promote international monetary cooperation and provide financial assistance to countries with balance of payments difficulties
options.C: Regulate international trade between WTO member countries
options.D: Provide grants to the poorest nations of the world

questions.explanation: The IMF promotes international monetary cooperation, exchange rate stability, and balanced growth of trade, and provides financial assistance to countries with balance of payments problems.

questions.question: Which of the following sequences CORRECTLY represents the flow of goods from producer to consumer?

options.A: Consumer → Retailer → Wholesaler → Producer
options.B: Producer → Wholesaler → Retailer → Consumer
options.C: Producer → Consumer → Retailer → Wholesaler
options.D: Retailer → Wholesaler → Producer → Consumer

questions.explanation: The typical chain of distribution is: Producer → Wholesaler → Retailer → Consumer. Aids to trade (transport, insurance, banking, warehousing) support this flow at every stage.
